the-mom-test.title: The Mom Test
the-mom-test.author: Rob Fitzpatrick
the-mom-test.providerPrompt: Create a text-free vertical icon illustration for a premium mobile learning app book tile. Concept seed: How to talk to customers and learn the truth when everyone is lying to you — including your mom. Reader situation: You are about to build something and want to find out whether anyone actually needs it, before you spend a year finding out the hard way. Required symbol: one microphone pointed at a small evidence dot. Required palette: solid teal background, yellow microphone, coral evidence dot. Style variation: Swiss poster abstraction. Use one huge geometric symbol, hard edges, asymmetry, and a dramatic diagonal or circular composition. It should feel more like a memorable poster crop than a generic app icon. Use bold color blocking and one surprising accent shape. Avoid centered sameness across books. Memorability standard: the image must read instantly at a tiny thumbnail size. If the idea needs more than one emblem, simplify it. Style: flat 2D illustration, bold geometric shapes, crisp edges, premium editorial taste, not photorealistic, not painterly, not collage. Composition standard: one centered emblem in the upper 56% of the 2:3 vertical canvas, generous negative space, no scene, no background environment, no depth-of-field, no realistic room, no hands. Simplicity limit: use one main object and at most one supporting shape. Avoid clusters, crowds, many lines, tiny parts, diagrams, dashboards, maps with detail, charts, documents, UI, and decorative clutter. Background standard: perfectly solid matte color field or a nearly solid field with only extremely subtle paper grain. No gradients behind the future title plate, no pattern fields, no texture-heavy backgrounds. Color standard: very high contrast and memorable. Use the required palette strongly so this book looks different from the others. Avoid beige, gray, washed-out neutrals, muddy gradients, monochrome palettes, and low-contrast pastel art. Hard no-text rule: no letters, no words, no book title, no author name, no numbers, no punctuation marks, no fake glyphs, no interface icons, no logos, no labels, no signage. Avoid literal speech bubbles, question marks, documents with writing, screens with UI, pages with text, book mockups, or any surface that could look like typography. Keep the bottom 44% visually calm because the website will place a solid metadata plate over that area. Do not create a physical book cover, 3D book object, product photo, border, page spine, drop shadow, room background, hands, or a book sitting on a surface.

thinking-fast-and-slow.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
thinking-fast-and-slow.author: Daniel Kahneman
thinking-fast-and-slow.providerPrompt: Create a text-free vertical icon illustration for a premium mobile learning app book tile. Concept seed: A map of the two systems that run your mind — and the predictable ways the fast one fools you. Reader situation: You make judgments under uncertainty for a living — hiring, investing, forecasting, designing — and want to know where your intuition is trustworthy and where it is confidently wrong. Required symbol: two simple paths, one lightning bolt and one slow curve. Required palette: solid burgundy background, yellow lightning path, cyan slow curve. Style variation: Swiss poster abstraction. Use one huge geometric symbol, hard edges, asymmetry, and a dramatic diagonal or circular composition. good-strategy-bad-strategy.title: Good Strategy Bad Strategy
good-strategy-bad-strategy.author: Richard Rumelt
good-strategy-bad-strategy.providerPrompt: Create a text-free vertical icon illustration for a premium mobile learning app book tile. Concept seed: Why strategy is not ambition, values, or a list of goals - it is a diagnosis, a guiding policy, and coherent action. deep-work.title: Deep Work
deep-work.author: Cal Newport
deep-work.providerPrompt: Create a text-free vertical icon illustration for a premium mobile learning app book tile. Concept seed: A practical argument that valuable work increasingly depends on protecting long, undistracted stretches of attention. Reader situation: Your day is full of Slack, email, meetings, and context switching, and the work that actually moves your life or company forward keeps getting postponed. Required symbol: one glowing desk lamp cone cutting through dark space. Required palette: solid midnight blue background, cyan lamp cone, warm yellow focus dot. Style variation: Swiss poster abstraction. Use one huge geometric symbol, hard edges, asymmetry, and a dramatic diagonal or circular composition.
